title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News
summary: SteelRadar provides comprehensive coverage of global iron and steel industry pricing, news, and analyses. Recent updates include reporting on scrap market upward movement with increased trading activity and cost pressure, as well as industry leadership engagement with young professionals at the 'From Experience to the Future' event.

title: Commodity Price Watch: March 2026
summary: Steel prices are higher in the US and Europe while remaining largely unchanged in most of Asia. The analysis highlights that weak demand will prevent full pass-through of higher input costs driven by natural gas disruptions and elevated energy prices from geopolitical tensions, with supply disruption risks tied to war-related shipping issues.

title: Long Product Price Hikes Persist
summary: Long products continue their upward momentum with significant price increases reported for wire rods and other steel products. The article discusses ongoing market dynamics affecting pricing in the steel industry.

title: US Scrap Pricing Moves Sideways; HRC Strength Continues
summary: US scrap pricing has stabilized with sideways movement while hot-rolled coil (HRC) prices continue to show strength. The report reflects market conditions in early March 2026.

title: Copper Scrap Prices Jump $0.09 | Daily Scrap Market
summary: Copper scrap prices led the market higher on March 25, 2026, with key grades such as number one and number two copper wire and bare bright rising $0.09 per pound on the ScrapMonster Price Index. COMEX copper closed near $5.49 per pound, supporting the upward move in scrap prices. This coordinated rise in futures and scrap typically bolsters yard pricing and steady selling flow.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel rose to 17,215 USD/T on March 27, 2026, up 0.29% from the previous day, rebounding from a recent over one-month low to around $17,400 per tonne. The increase is driven by hopes for a temporary de-escalation in the Middle East conflict following a US-proposed one-month ceasefire to Iran, boosting risk sentiment. Additionally, Nickel Industries received approval for 14.3 million wet metric tonnes of 2026 nickel ore sales in Indonesia, supporting supply clarity and futures prices.

title: Nickel Demand for EVs Could Flip the 2030 Market Balance
summary: Nickel prices advanced 2.35% to $17,537.64 per ton globally and ¥120,970 in China, driven by renewed macro factors despite a current market surplus projected at 156,000 tonnes in 2026 by S&P Global. Indonesian smelters maintain high utilization using stockpiles and imports, leading to rising LME inventories at 275,634 tonnes in January 2026. Future demand from EVs could shift balance, potentially pushing prices toward $25,000 per tonne as inventories drop.

title: Middle East war pressures copper, exposes nickel risk
summary: LME 3M nickel prices rallied within the $17,000/mt to $18,000/mt range, partly due to Indonesia's recent quota adjustments amid Middle East conflict pressures. The conflict has increased energy prices, weakening global growth and copper demand while strengthening the US dollar. This adds downward pressure on industrial metals, with nickel facing supply risks highlighted by the situation.

title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers Mar-26-2026
summary: Nickel prices slipped to $17,165-$17,290/tonne on March 26, down 0.3%-1.0%, in a risk-off pattern after a brief rebound, remaining 5.7%-6.5% higher year-over-year. Support is mainly policy-driven from Indonesia's production restraint, making it vulnerable to cautious sentiment. Earlier rallies to $17,900/tonne were not due to improved end demand.

title: Nickel Futures Rise from Over 1-Month Low
summary: Nickel futures rose to around $17,400-$17,500 per tonne from an over one-month low, fueled by hopes for Middle East de-escalation via a US-proposed ceasefire to Iran. Supply clarity from Nickel Industries' 14.3 million tonne nickel ore sales approval in Indonesia underpins prices, though caution persists over potential Indonesian windfall taxes. Prices had slid to $17,000 per tonne amid rising global risk aversion.

title: TIME Recognizes ZincFive Among America's Top GreenTech Companies 2026
summary: ZincFive has been named to TIME's America's Top GreenTech Companies 2026 list for the third consecutive year, recognizing its nickel-zinc (NiZn) battery solutions for immediate power applications. The technology offers high power density, inherent safety, and sustainable performance for data centers and critical infrastructure. Ranked #142, it is one of only two Oregon-headquartered companies on the list.

title: Middle East war pressures copper, exposes nickel risk
summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including potential disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, highlight vulnerabilities in the nickel supply chain for high-pressure acid leach (HPAL) operations used in battery-grade nickel sulfate production. HPAL relies on sulfuric acid from sulfur inputs, and disruptions could lead to supply shortages and price volatility. Indonesia's HPAL plants may benefit from lower costs, while others face higher risks.

title: Porous Nickel Plates Market Outlook 2026-2034
summary: The porous nickel plates market is growing due to demand in advanced battery technologies, fuel cells, and renewable energy storage like nickel-metal hydride batteries and electrolyzers for green hydrogen. These plates provide high conductivity, durability, and improved efficiency in charge-discharge processes and electrochemical reactions. Manufacturers are optimizing pore structures for next-generation applications in electric vehicles and supercapacitors.

title: Tenaris supports HRSG projects worldwide to meet rising electricity demand
summary: Tenaris is supporting complex heat recovery steam generator (HRSG) projects worldwide, securing agreements for 15 projects in 2025 totaling over 17,000 tons of pipes produced at its Silcotub mill in Romania. The company leverages its integrated supply chain to meet tight schedules and high-quality standards. It will showcase solutions at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy.

title: Tenaris to supply line pipe, OCTG, coating and services for Sakarya Phase 3 in offshore Turkey
summary: Tenaris has been awarded contracts to supply seamless and welded pipes, coatings, and services for the Sakarya gas field Phase 3 offshore Turkey, including pipes produced at its Dalmine mill in Italy and coated in Villamarzana. The project involves flowlines, a MEG pipeline, export pipeline, and risers under the One Line® service model. Additionally, 10,000 tons of casing with Dopeless® technology will support offshore drilling.

title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
summary: Tenaris will showcase its hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy, from March 24-27. The products, supported by its integrated supply chain including the Piombino facility in Italy, are used in firefighting, water/gas transport, and heating/cooling systems. Recently certified galvanized, epoxy-coated, and polyethylene-coated pipes have Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs).

title: Prysmian creates the world's first negative-carbon-footprint cable
summary: Prysmian announced the development of the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint using a cradle-to-gate approach where raw material procurement and manufacturing remove more CO2 emissions than they generate. The new cables can integrate into existing grids and will debut within Prysmian's Power Grid business in the second half of 2026.

title: Prysmian develops world's first cable with negative carbon footprint
summary: Prysmian Spa announced the development of the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint, designed to significantly reduce carbon emissions through cutting-edge engineering systems and next-generation low-carbon and recycled materials. The technology is expected to debut in the Power Grid business in the second half of 2026.
